description_en: An inscribed stone stupa (itabi) classified as archaeological material and a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located at Ōaza Katsuse 723-1, on the grounds of Gokoku-ji.

name_en: Inscribed Stone Stupa Dated the Fourth Year of Kenchō
description_en: An inscribed stone stupa (itabi) classified as archaeological material and a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located at Ōaza Nambata-shinden 83.

name_romaji: Kitadōri iseki dai-hachigō hōkei shūkōbo shutsudo ibutsu
name_en: Artifacts Excavated from Square Moated Burial No. 8 at the Kitadōri Site
description_en: Archaeological artifacts (tangible cultural property) excavated from a square moated burial at the Kitadōri Site. They are municipally designated in Fujimi City. They are held at the Mizuko Shell Mound Museum.
Pottery of the Uchikoshi type, classified as archaeological material and a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is held at the Mizuko Shell Mound Museum.

name_en: Bronze Bowl Excavated from the Hikawamae Site
description_en: A bronze bowl classified as archaeological material and a tangible cultural property, excavated from the Hikawamae Site. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is held at the Fujimi City Cultural Property Repository.

name_en: Former Ōsawa Family Residence, Main House
description_en: The main house of the former Ōsawa family residence, a structure classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located within Nanbata Castle Park.
The front gate of the Ōsawa family residence, a structure classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located in Ōaza Higashi-Ōkubo.
The grain storehouse of the Ōsawa family residence, a structure classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located in Ōaza Higashi-Ōkubo.

name_en: Former Kaneko Family Residence, Main House
description_en: The main house of the former Kaneko family residence, a structure classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located within Nanbata Castle Park.

The row-house gate (nagayamon) of the former Suzuki family, a structure classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located within Nanbata Castle Park.
A sluice gate (montoi) classified as a structure and tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located at Ōaza Kami-Nambata 295-3.
A culvert (hikan) classified as a structure and tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located at Ōaza Shimo-Nambata 125-3.

name_en: Seated Statue of En no Gyōja
description_en: A seated statue of En no Gyōja, classified as sculpture and a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located at Ōaza Mizuko 1762-3, Suimiya Shrine.
Historical documents (komonjo) of the Yokota family, classified as a tangible cultural property. They are municipally designated in Fujimi City. They are held at the Nanbata Castle Museum.
Historical documents of the Yagishita family Jūgyokuin, classified as a tangible cultural property. They are municipally designated in Fujimi City. They are held at the Nanbata Castle Museum.
Historical documents of the Hannya-in of Suimiya Shrine, classified as a tangible cultural property. They are municipally designated in Fujimi City. They are located at Ōaza Mizuko 1762.
Historical documents of the Ōsawa family, classified as a tangible cultural property. They are municipally designated in Fujimi City. They are held at the Nanbata Castle Museum.
Historical documents of the Hayashi family, classified as a tangible cultural property. They are municipally designated in Fujimi City. They are held at the Nanbata Castle Museum.

name_en: Lion Dance of Nambata Hachiman Shrine
description_en: A lion dance (shishimai) classified as an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is maintained by the Nambata Hachiman Shrine Lion Dance Preservation Society.

name_en: Lion Dance of Tsuruma Suwa Shrine
description_en: A lion dance (shishimai) classified as an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is maintained by the Watado Lion Dance Group.
Festival music (hayashi) classified as an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is maintained by the Katsuse-bayashi Preservation Society.
Festival music (hayashi) classified as an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is maintained by the Mizuko Kamigumi-bayashi ensemble.
Festival music (hayashi) classified as an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is maintained by the Mizuko Shironoshita-gumi-bayashi ensemble.
Festival music (hayashi) classified as an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is maintained by the Mizuko Ishii-bayashi Preservation Society.
Festival music (hayashi) classified as an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is maintained by the Naka-Mizuko-bayashi Preservation Society.
A zelkova tree designated as a natural monument. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It stands at Suwa 2-1589-1, on the grounds of Suwa Shrine.
A ginkgo tree designated as a natural monument. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It stands at Ōaza Katsuse 791-1, on the grounds of Haruna Shrine.
A kaya (Japanese nutmeg-yew) tree designated as a natural monument. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It stands at Suwa 1-8-3, on the grounds of Rurikō-ji.

name_en: Earthworks of the Nanbata Castle Ruins
description_en: Earthen ramparts (dorui) of the Nanbata Castle ruins, designated as a historic site. It is municipally designated in Fujimi City. It is located in Ōaza Shimo-Nambata.

A Buddhist triad of statues at Kōmyō-ji, classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1964-09-08. It is located at Honchō 14-43, Kazo City.

name_en: Seated Statue of Fudō Myōō by Enkū
description_en: A seated statue of Fudō Myōō attributed to Enkū, classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1980-03-12. It is located at Chūō 2-7-12, Kazo City.
A stone talisman marker (ishigantō) classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1956-09-24. It is located at Chūō 2-5-27, Kazo City.

A festival float and a mask of Ranryōō, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1972-06-17. It is located at Honchō 7-17, Kazo City.
A black gate (kuromon) classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1960-09-08. It is located at Fudōoka 2-9-18, Kazo City.

The Fudō hall of Sōgan-ji, a structure classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1977-06-09. It is located at Fudōoka 2-9-18, Kazo City.
A Kurikara Fudō sword classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1955-07-30. It is located at Fudōoka 2-9-18, Kazo City.

name_en: Slab Stone Stupa with Scattered-Lotus Design
description_en: A slab stone stupa (itaishi tōba) decorated with a scattered-lotus design, classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1955-07-30. It is located at Fudōoka 2-9-18, Kazo City.

A bluestone stupa (tōba) carved with the Thirteen Buddhas, classified as a tangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1980-06-07. It is located at Fudōoka 973, Kazo City.
A haiku monument commemorating the poet Bashō, designated as a historic site. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1955-07-30. It is located at Fudōoka 2-9-18, Kazo City.
The site marking the origin of Fudōoka High School, designated as a historic site. It is municipally designated in Kazo City and was designated on 1985-01-10. It is located at Fudōoka 3-4, Kazo City.
